iterations.title: Confirm the hook mechanism and the carve-outs for a repo-local commit gate
Pin the commit-match rule, deny wording, and plan the hook + CLAUDE.md
E1 - implement the commit-gate hook, wire settings.json, dry-run both states

plan.sketch: E1 - Implement the commit gate: write .claude/hooks/require-workstream-commit.py (stdlib python3; reads stdin JSON; if tool is Bash and command contains a git commit invocation, allow only when a .lume/workstreams/*/state.json has workstream.status active AND its latest iteration phase == working, else deny with a reason; non-Bash and non-commit commands pass), wire it into .claude/settings.json PreToolUse matcher Bash, and dry-run the decision logic against both states.

E2 - Write repo-root CLAUDE.md documenting the way of working: all repo changes go through a lume workstream; how to start/drive one; the commit-only gate and its no-deadlock bootstrap path; the repo-only / not-a-lume-feature boundary; that the operator's manual edits are not policed.
Closeout - verify done-when, retro (did the gate buy back more than its ceremony), confirm nothing under plugin/ changed and no engine code touched; hand off for operator accept + PR merge.

workstream.title: Enforce: all repo changes go through a lume workstream (repo-only commit gate)
